title: Add Laravel x-api-key middleware for mobile API routes
goal: Add an app-level API key gate for all future mobile API routes without changing the existing web frontend routes.

context.current_auth: Laravel Sanctum Bearer tokens are already used by the existing API.

implementation_steps: Read the expected key from config('services.mobile.api_key').
Read the provided key from the request header x-api-key.
If the configured key is empty, missing, or does not match, return JSON status 401.
Use hash_equals for safe string comparison.
Register middleware alias mobile.api.key in Backend/bootstrap/app.php.
Add a mobile config entry to Backend/config/services.php.
Do not add the real key to committed files.
Do not apply the middleware to existing /api routes yet.

middleware_behavior.valid_response: Request continues to the next middleware/controller.

acceptance_criteria: Alias mobile.api.key is registered in bootstrap/app.php.
Requests with an incorrect x-api-key return 401 JSON.
Requests with the correct x-api-key continue normally.
Existing Frontend API behavior is unchanged.
verification: Run php artisan config:clear from Backend.
Use a temporary test route or later mobile route to verify missing x-api-key returns 401.
Verify existing /api/login still works without x-api-key.
out_of_scope: Do not create mobile routes in this task.
Do not modify existing AuthController behavior in this task.
